title: Undergraduate Admission - Applying to Queen's
content: If you're ready to apply, there are a few ways you can! Not sure what application is right for you? Check below for eligibility for each application.
source: Queen's University - Undergraduate Admission and Recruitment

title: Application Methods
content: Three ways to apply: 1. Ontario Universities' Application Centre (OUAC): For applicants from within Canada or those applying to another university in Ontario. 2. Common App ApplicationCentre: For applicants from the United States or outside Canada wanting to use Common App. 3. Queen's International Application Portal: For international applicants not applying to another Ontario University.
source: Queen's University - Undergraduate Admission and Recruitment

title: Apply Online - OUAC
content: Apply using the OUAC application. After applying, you will receive an acknowledgement email with an 8-digit Queen's student number and instructions on how to activate your NetID (5–7 business days).
source: Queen's University - Undergraduate Admission and Recruitment

title: Submit Supplementary Information - OUAC
content: For students applying to Commerce, Nursing or Health Sciences, a Supplementary Application is required. Students applying to Bachelor of Music, Concurrent Education Music, and the Bachelor of Music Theatre programs are required to audition.
source: Queen's University - Undergraduate Admission and Recruitment

title: Check Your To-Do List - OUAC
content: Your To-Do list will be found on your SOLUS student centre. You can access your SOLUS account once you activate your NetID. Each To-Do list is different for each applicant.
source: Queen's University - Undergraduate Admission and Recruitment

title: Apply Online - Common App
content: Apply using the Common App application. After applying, you will receive an acknowledgement email with an 8-digit Queen's student number and instructions on how to activate your NetID (5–7 business days).
source: Queen's University - Undergraduate Admission and Recruitment

title: Submit Supplementary Information - Common App
content: For students applying to Commerce, Nursing or Health Sciences, a Supplementary Application is required. Students applying to Bachelor of Music, Concurrent Education Music, and the Bachelor of Music Theatre programs are required to audition.
source: Queen's University - Undergraduate Admission and Recruitment

title: Check Your To-Do List - Common App
content: Your To-Do list will be found on your SOLUS student centre. You can access your SOLUS account once you activate your NetID. Each To-Do list is different for each applicant.
source: Queen's University - Undergraduate Admission and Recruitment

title: Apply Online - Queen's International Application
content: Apply using the Queen's University International Application Portal.
source: Queen's University - Undergraduate Admission and Recruitment

title: Submit Supplementary Information - Queen's International Application
content: For students applying to Commerce, Nursing or Health Sciences, a Supplementary Application is required. Students applying to Bachelor of Music, Concurrent Education Music, and the Bachelor of Music Theatre programs are required to audition.
source: Queen's University - Undergraduate Admission and Recruitment

title: Check Your To-Do List - Queen's International Application
content: Your To-Do list can be found on your applicant portal. It may be different for each application, so be sure to explore the required items for each application submitted.
source: Queen's University - Undergraduate Admission and Recruitment

title: Waitlist FAQ - General Info
content: The waitlist is a pool of qualified applicants. It is not ranked in numerical order, and we are unable to tell you where you are on the waitlist. We will do everything we can to update you on your waitlist status as soon as possible. You will hear about the final outcome of your application by mid-June.

title: Waitlist FAQ - Offers and Acceptance
content: Accepting an offer to another program will not impact your chances of being admitted from the waitlist. If you receive an offer of admission from the waitlist at Queen’s, you can accept your new offer. If you wish to be added to the waitlist, please go to your SOLUS Student Centre (my.queensu.ca), click the Admissions tile to select the program, and then click ACCEPT. Your response must be received by 11:59 PM ET on the date indicated in your email inviting you to be on the waitlist.

title: Waitlist FAQ - Waitlist Process
content: We will move as quickly as possible to fill additional spaces that may remain in the program. There is no guarantee that any potential waitlist offers will be made prior to the deadlines you have to accept other offers of admission. If you have another offer, you can (and should) accept it by the deadline. Should you receive an offer from the waitlist, you will need to cancel any prior acceptance before accepting the new offer from the waitlist.

title: Waitlist FAQ - Residence Guarantee
content:
Queen’s will guarantee a space in residence for:
- Students who receive an offer of admission to Queen’s on or before April 15, 2025
- International students who receive an offer of admission to Queen’s on or before June 1, 2025
- Indigenous students who receive an offer of admission to Queen’s on or before June 1, 2025
It is anticipated that 90%+ of all offers of admission will include a residence guarantee. You can apply to residence before you accept your offer of admission.
To remain eligible for the guarantee, you must apply for residence and pay the deposit by the application deadline of June 2, 2025, at 4 pm ET.

title: Waitlist FAQ - Residence Lottery
content:
If you receive an offer of admission after April 15, 2025, that does not contain a residence guarantee, and you apply for residence and pay the deposit by the deadline of June 2, 2025, at 4 pm ET, then you will be placed in a lottery for access to residence. You will be notified by June 10 whether you have a residence space or have been placed on the waitlist.
If you receive an offer of admission after June 2, 2025, you may apply to the residence waitlist. Residence offers will continue to be made, as spaces become available over the summer.
Refer to your offer letter from Undergraduate Admission and Recruitment if you are not sure if residence is guaranteed for you. Additional information about Queen’s University’s Residence Commitment is available here.

title: Waitlist FAQ - Scholarships and Bursaries
content: If you are admitted from the waitlist, you are eligible for Queen’s scholarships and bursaries. If you are awarded a scholarship and/or a bursary, you will be notified when you receive your offer of admission.

title: Waitlist FAQ - Missed Deadline
content: If you missed the deadline to accept your spot on the waitlist, please contact our office as soon as possible at admresp@queensu.ca. Your request will be reviewed by our Admission Committee, and you will receive an update within 1 business day.

title: Waitlist FAQ - Correction Request
content: If you meant to accept your spot on the waitlist, but you accidentally clicked decline in SOLUS, please contact our office as soon as possible to request a correction at admresp@queensu.ca. Our Admission Committee will review your request, and you will receive an update within 1 business day.

title: English Proficiency Requirements - Overview
content: If you did not study in an English-speaking country or if English is not your first language, you must prove your English proficiency through an English language test or the QBridge Language Pathway programs. If an English Language test score is required, you will find it listed in your To Do List in your SOLUS student account. If you have been asked to submit an English language test and believe this does not apply to you, you may request a waiver. Queen’s University reserves the right to request an English language test result from any applicant at any time, when necessary.

title: English Proficiency Requirements - Acceptable Tests
content:
The minimum acceptable scores for English proficiency tests for admission to Queen's undergraduate programs are:
- IELTS Academic: 6.5 overall, no band below 6.0
- TOEFL iBT: 88 overall; 20 in Writing, Speaking, Reading, Listening
- TOEFL PBT: 580 (TBC), Institution Code 0949
- CAEL: 70 overall, no band below 60
- Pearson PTE Academic: 60 overall
- Duolingo English Test: 120 minimum
- Cambridge English: 175 overall, no component below 170
- LanguageCert Academic: 70 overall, no individual score below 65
Test results must be submitted via the testing body and are valid for two years.

title: English Proficiency Requirements - Submission Guidelines
content: To match your test results with your application, ensure you use the same information as your Queen’s application: first and last name, date of birth, and gender. Only official results sent directly from the testing body are acceptable.

title: English Proficiency Requirements - QBridge Programs
content:
QBridge Language Pathway Programs are for students who do not meet the required English language proficiency but are academically competitive for admission. There are two pathways:
- QBridge English for Academic Purposes (EAP): For students not eligible for Accelerated; may require Fall and Winter terms, and possibly a Summer term if standards are not met.
- QBridge Accelerated: Intensive 8-week summer course starting in early July; must achieve at least a B grade to progress to degree studies in September. QBridge ensures students are linguistically, academically, and culturally prepared for undergraduate study.

title: English Proficiency Requirements - Waivers
content:
Applicants may request a waiver if they:
- Completed an IB diploma (SL English B insufficient)
- Lived in an English-speaking country for the last 3 years prior to university
- Attended a full-time English-medium institution for the last 3 years prior to university
To request a waiver, contact admission@queensu.ca with school details, principal contact info, and supporting documentation showing English as the medium of instruction. Queen’s will review and respond; if a waiver is not granted, an acceptable English test score must be submitted by March 31st.

title: QBridge Accelerated (QBA) - Entry Requirements
content:
Term: Fall (September start) | Winter (January start)
Entry requirements:
- IELTS: 6.0 overall
- Cambridge English: 170
- TOEFL iBT: 80
- CAEL: Overall Band 60 or equivalent
- Duolingo: 95
- PTE: 55
- MELAB: 78
- LanguageCert Academic: Minimum band score of 65
Graduated Entry-exit requirements:
- B+ in ESLA 140 (Advanced level), or
- B- in ESLA 150 (University Preparation level)
Exit requirements*:
- A final grade of B at the end of the summer program

title: English for Academic Purposes (EAP) - Entry Requirements
content:
Term: Fall (September start) | Winter (January start)
Entry requirements:
- IELTS: 5.5 overall
- Cambridge English: 160
- TOEFL iBT: 58
- CAEL: Overall Band 50 or equivalent
- Duolingo: 80
- PTE: 40
- MELAB: 58
- LanguageCert Academic: Minimum band score of 60
Graduated Entry-exit requirements:
- A final grade of B- at the end of the summer program
Exit requirements*:
- A- in ESLA 140 (Advanced level), or
- B in ESLA 150 (University Preparation level)

text: Queen's University offers a variety of undergraduate programs for Winter (January) Entry, including Arts, Science, Computing, and Health Sciences. Programs are designed to fit students' academic and professional goals.

metadata.faculty: Arts and Science, Health Sciences, Computing

text: Honours Programs are typically 4 years with a major-focused curriculum. General Programs are typically 3 years, offering a broader, concentration-based approach.

metadata.faculty: Arts and Science, Health Sciences, Computing

text: Available Majors/Concentrations include: Art History, Classics and Archaeology, Computing and the Creative Arts, Economics, Employment Relations, English Literature and Creative Writing, Environmental Studies, Film and Media, Gender Studies, Geography and Planning, Global Development Studies, Health Studies, History, Languages, Literatures and Cultures, Philosophy, Political Studies, Religious Studies, Sociology, Biology, Environmental Science, Geological Sciences, Mathematics and Statistics, Physics, Artificial Intelligence, Cognitive Science, Computing, Math and Analytics, Fundamental Computation, Security, Software Design. Health Science applicants must submit a Supplementary Application.

metadata.faculty: Arts and Science, Health Sciences, Computing

text: Winter Entry admission is only open to international students who require a study permit. Eligibility includes: never attended a post-secondary institution, attempted less than 24.0 units at another university and not currently enrolled in fall courses, or attended college but not graduating from a 3-year advanced diploma and not enrolled in fall courses.

metadata.faculty: Arts and Science, Health Sciences, Computing

text: Applicants not eligible for First-year Winter Entry include: University Transfer Applicants with 24.0 or more units at another institution, College Students graduating from a three-year diploma program, and Exchange Applicants who should contact their home universities.

metadata.faculty: Arts and Science, Health Sciences, Computing

text: To apply: create an account with first name, last name, and email, confirm email, reset password, and start the application. Separate applications are required for multiple degrees, each with a 50 CAD fee.

metadata.faculty: Arts and Science, Health Sciences, Computing
